commands.review_summary:
This PR adds an opt-in Telegram native `sendMessageDraft` path for transient private-DM tool-progress updates, new `streaming.preview` config fields, Telegram docs/maintainer-note updates, and regression tests.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction: this is a new optional Telegram transport feature. Source inspection confirms current main only uses the edited preview stream for tool progress, while live native-draft behavior still needs inspectable proof.
commands.review_followup: This PR needs contributor-supplied real Telegram proof and maintainer product review for the new Telegram-only streaming config before any repair automation would be useful.

target.title: [codex] Add gated Telegram native DM tool-progress drafts
target.body:
## Summary

This PR adds an optional Telegram-native `sendMessageDraft` lane for transient DM tool-progress / Thinking previews.

The important boundary: this does **not** stream final answer text through native Telegram drafts. Final answers, reasoning text, media, buttons, errors, and approval prompts continue to use the normal persistent delivery path. Native drafts are only used for short-lived tool-progress/status updates before answer text begins.

## Why

Telegram DMs can get noisy during tool-heavy turns because transient progress/status updates currently have to share the same visible edited preview lane as answer text. Native Telegram drafts let supported clients show that interim status as ephemeral UI instead of persisting tool chatter into chat history.

This gives Telegram DM users a cleaner live status experience while preserving the existing durable final-message behavior.

## Safety model

- Default off: `streaming.preview.nativeToolProgress` must be explicitly enabled.
- Optional canary allowlist: `streaming.preview.nativeToolProgressAllowFrom` limits rollout by Telegram user/chat ID.
- DM-only: groups, supergroups, room events, and forum topics do not create native draft transports.
- Non-blocking fallback: if `sendMessageDraft` is unavailable or rejected, OpenClaw falls back to the existing edited preview behavior.
- Durable payload guardrails: media, errors, inline buttons, and exec approvals are never hidden in ephemeral native drafts.
- Answer-streaming boundary is documented in the Telegram maintainer notes so future changes do not reintroduce native drafts for final answers.

## Config example

```json
{
  "channels": {
    "telegram": {
      "streaming": {
        "mode": "partial",
        "preview": {
          "toolProgress": true,
          "nativeToolProgress": true,
          "nativeToolProgressAllowFrom": ["123456789"]
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

## Validation

- Live Telegram canary with a separate test bot: native draft was visible in a private DM, and the final answer arrived as a normal persistent Telegram message.
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/telegram/src/native-tool-progress-draft.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`
  - 2 files / 78 tests passed
- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/config/zod-schema.providers-core.ts src/config/types.base.ts`
  - 143 files / 1425 tests passed
- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/doctor.test.ts src/config/schema.test.ts`
  - 3 shards passed
- `pnpm config:channels:check`

Note: `codex review --base origin/main` could not complete locally because the Codex CLI auth path returned `401 Unauthorized`. I did a manual review pass afterwards and fixed the generated bundled channel config metadata drift it exposed via `pnpm config:channels:check`.
target.author: akrimm702
